notes: Khasi uprising. Tirot Sing, also known as U Tirot Sing Syiem, was a Khasi chief in the early 19th century. The Khasi uprising (Anglo-Khasi War) was a conflict between the Khasi people and the British Empire that took place from 1829 to 1833. The Sanyasi Uprisings took place in Bengal between the periods of 1770- 1820s. Poligar Revolt fought between the British and the Poligars, feudal lords in southern India. The Singpho Rebellion of 1830 took place in Assam. Q.675. The planned large-scale disruption of rail communications and the seizure of arms in Bengal were undertaken under the leadership of _____. SSC MTS 13/11/2024 (3rd Shift) (a) Aurobindo Ghose (b) Surendranath Banerjee (c) Chittaranjan Das (d) Jatin Mukherjee Sol.675.(d) Jatin Mukherjee, also known as Bagha Jatin, was a prominent revolutionary leader in Bengal during the early 20th century who fought against British colonial rule. He was a key member of the Anushilan Samiti, a revolutionary organization that planned History large-scale armed resistance against the British.

Surya Sen is also known as Master Da. He founded The Indian Republican Army. The Chittagong Armoury Raid took place on April 18, 1930, in Chittagong (now in Bangladesh). Ras Bihari Bose was the founder of the 'Indian Independence League’. He chaired the Indian Independence League in 1942 to formally launch the Indian National Army.

Santhal Rebellion (Hul revolt) began in 1855, led by Sidhu, Kanhu, Chand, Bhairav, and their sisters Phulo and Jhano. The Malabar rebellion (Moplah rebellion) was an armed uprising by the Mappila Muslims of Kerala in 1921. The Kol rebellion (1829-39) was led by Buddhu Bhagat, Madara Mahato, and Joa Bhagat in the Chota Nagpur region. The Munda Rebellion, led by Birsa Munda, took place in the Ranchi area in 1899-1900.

1911. Vinayak Damodar Savarkar was sent to the Cellular Jail on the punishment of 'Kala Pani’ under the Nashik Conspiracy Case for the murder of the Collector of Nashik District, Jackson. While serving his sentence in the Cellular Jail, he wrote the epic 'Kamala' on the prison walls.

Tarak Nath Das and GD Kumar. They were Indian revolutionaries who collaborated to promote Indian independence from British rule. Shyamji Krishna Varma founded the Indian Home Rule Society, India House, and The Indian Sociologist in London. Vinayak Damodar Savarkar and his brother Ganesh Damodar Savarkar established the Abhinav Bharat Society, also known as the Young India Society, in 1904. Q.680. Which of the following groups/parties was started in San Francisco, United States of America during the Indian National Movement? SSC CGL 26/09/2024 (2nd Shift) (a) Hindustan Socialist Republican Association (b) Swaraj Party (c) Khilafat Committee (d) Ghadar Party Sol.680.(d) Ghadar Party : Founded in 1913 in the U.S. as the Hindi Association of the Pacific Coast, it aimed to free India from British rule. It was founded by Sohan Singh Bhakna and Lala Har Dayal.

Sanyasi Rebellion. The Sanyasi Uprisings took place in Bengal between the periods of 1770- 1820s. The Sanyasis rose in rebellion after the great famine of 1770 in Bengal which caused acute chaos and misery. However, the immediate cause of the rebellion was the restrictions imposed by the British upon pilgrims visiting holy places among both Hindus and Muslims. The national song of India, 'Vande Mataram’, is an excerpt taken from the novel Anand Math written by Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay. Q.682. Who founded the Bombay Presidency Association in 1885 along with Pherozeshah Mehta and KT Telang? SSC CHSL 09/07/2024 (2nd shift) (a) AK Fazlul Haq (b) Badruddin Tyabji (c) Behramji M Malabari (d) Zakir Husain Sol.682.(b) Badruddin Tyabji was the third President (in 1887) of the Indian National Congress and the first Muslim to hold that position. Other Political organisations and founder: London Indian Society (1865) - Dadabhai Naoroji. East India Association (1866) - Dadabhai Naoroji. Poona Sarvajanik Sabha (1870) - G.V. Joshi, S.H. Sathe, S.H. Chiplonkar and Mahadev Govind Ranade. Q.683. What was the name of the campaign led by the Ali brothers and Maulana Abul Kalam Azad to save the Ottoman Caliphate as a symbol of unity among the Muslim community? SSC CHSL 09/07/2024 (3rd shift) (a) Faraizi Movement (b) Ahmadiya Movement (c) Wahabi Movement (d) Khilafat Movement Sol.683.(d) Khilafat Movement. It was a political campaign that took place from 1919 to 1922 in British India. It was a protest against the British government's policies towards Turkey and the planned dismantling of the Ottoman Empire after World War I. The Faraizi movement was started by Haji Shariatullah in 1818. The Ahmadiyya movement was founded by Mirza Ghulam Ahmad in 1889. Syed Ahmed Barelvi started the Wahabi Movement in the 1820s. Q.684. Who was executed as an accomplice to the murder of AMT Jackson ? SSC CPO 29/06/2024 (1st shift) (a) Krishnaji Gopal Karve (b) Matangini Hazra (c) Khudiram Bose (d) Hemu Kalani Sol.684.(a) Krishnaji Gopal Karve. Jackson murder case also known as Nasik Conspiracy (Year 1909). Anant Laxman Kanhere shot Jackson, the Collector of Nashik, at Vijayanand theatre. Kanhere was a member of the Abhinav Bharat society. The event led to the arrest of Savarkar. Abhinav Bharat Society was a secret society founded by Vinayak Damodar Savarkar and his brother Ganesh Damodar Savarkar in 1904. Q.685. Which of the following revolutionists was associated with the Anushilan Samiti? SSC CPO 29/06/2024 (3rd shift) (a) Rajguru (b) Bhagat Singh (c) Jatindra Mohan Sengupta (d) Sukhdev Sol.685.(c) Jatindra Mohan Sengupta. Anushilan Samiti was founded by Satish Chandra Bose and Barrister Praanath Mitra. Bhagat Singh, Rajguru, and Sukhdev were members of the Hindustan Socialist Republican Association (HSRA), involved in the Lahore Conspiracy Case of 1929.
